3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""
4	
5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler
14	
15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
16	
17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled
138	
139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187	
188	    def apply_penalties(
189	        self,
190	        logits: torch.Tensor,
191	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:
194	            assert sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids is not None
195	            logits = apply_all_penalties(
196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,
199	                sampling_metadata.frequency_penalties,
200	                sampling_metadata.repetition_penalties,
201	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
202	            )
203	        return logits
204	
205	    def apply_allowed_token_ids(
206	        self,
207	        logits: torch.Tensor,
208	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
209	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
210	        if sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None:
211	            logits.masked_fill_(sampling_metadata.allowed_token_ids_mask,
212	                                float("-inf"))
213	        return logits
214	
215	    def apply_bad_words(
216	        self,
217	        logits: torch.Tensor,
218	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
219	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
220	        if sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids:
221	            apply_bad_words(
222	                logits,
223	                sampling_metadata.bad_words_token_ids,
224	                sampling_metadata.output_token_ids,
225	            )
226	        return logits
227

600	    def _make_sampling_metadata(self) -> SamplingMetadata:
601	        num_reqs = self.num_reqs
602	        if not self.all_greedy:
603	            temperature = copy_slice(self.temperature_cpu_tensor,
604	                                     self.temperature, num_reqs)
605	        else:
606	            temperature = None
607	        if not self.no_top_p:
608	            copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)
609	        if not self.no_top_k:
610	            copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)
611	
612	        if not self.no_penalties:
613	            # Since syncing these tensors is expensive only copy them
614	            # if necessary i.e. if there are requests which require
615	            # penalties to be applied during sampling.
616	            copy_slice(self.frequency_penalties_cpu_tensor,
617	                       self.frequency_penalties, num_reqs)
618	            copy_slice(self.presence_penalties_cpu_tensor,
619	                       self.presence_penalties, num_reqs)
620	            copy_slice(self.repetition_penalties_cpu_tensor,
621	                       self.repetition_penalties, num_reqs)
622	
623	        needs_prompt_token_ids = (not self.no_penalties or
624	                                  (self.num_reqs > 0
625	                                   and self.logits_processing_needs_token_ids))
626	        if needs_prompt_token_ids:
627	            # The prompt tokens are used only for applying penalties or
628	            # step pooling during the sampling/pooling process.
629	            # Hence copy these tensors only when there are requests which
630	            # need penalties/step_pooler to be applied.
631	            prompt_token_ids = self._make_prompt_token_ids_tensor()
632	        else:
633	            prompt_token_ids = None
634	
635	        allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
636	        if not self.no_allowed_token_ids:
637	            assert self.allowed_token_ids_mask is not None
638	            copy_slice(self.allowed_token_ids_mask_cpu_tensor,
639	                       self.allowed_token_ids_mask, num_reqs)
640	            allowed_token_ids_mask = self.allowed_token_ids_mask[:num_reqs]
641	
642	        return SamplingMetadata(
643	            temperature=temperature,
644	            all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
645	            all_random=self.all_random,
646	            top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],
647	            top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],
648	            generators=self.generators,
649	            max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
650	            prompt_token_ids=prompt_token_ids,
651	            frequency_penalties=self.frequency_penalties[:num_reqs],
652	            presence_penalties=self.presence_penalties[:num_reqs],
653	            repetition_penalties=self.repetition_penalties[:num_reqs],
654	            output_token_ids=cast(list[list[int]], self.req_output_token_ids),
655	            no_penalties=self.no_penalties,
656	            allowed_token_ids_mask=allowed_token_ids_mask,
657	            bad_words_token_ids=self.bad_words_token_ids,
658	            logitsprocs=self.logitsprocs,
659	        )
660	
661	    @property
662	    def pooling_metadata(self) -> PoolingMetadata:
663	        if len(self.pooling_params) == 0:
664	            pooling_params = []
665	        else:
666	            # Note, for now this assumes that all request in the batch
667	            # are either sampling or pooling requests
668	            assert len(self.req_ids) == len(self.pooling_params)
669	            pooling_params = [
670	                self.pooling_params[req_id] for req_id in self.req_ids
671	            ]
672	
673	        return PoolingMetadata(
674	            prompt_lens=torch.from_numpy(
675	                self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs]).to(self.device),
676	            prompt_token_ids=self.sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
677	            pooling_params=pooling_params,
678	        )
679	
680	    def _make_prompt_token_ids_tensor(self) -> torch.Tensor:
681	        max_prompt_len = self.num_prompt_tokens[:self.num_reqs].max()
682	        prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor = torch.empty(
683	            (self.num_reqs, max_prompt_len),
684	            device="cpu",
685	            dtype=torch.int64,
686	            pin_memory=self.pin_memory,
687	        )
688	        prompt_token_ids = prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.numpy()
689	        prompt_token_ids[:] = self.token_ids_cpu[:self.
690	                                                 num_reqs, :max_prompt_len]
691	        # Use the value of vocab_size as a pad since we don't have a
692	        # token_id of this value.
693	        for i in range(self.num_reqs):
694	            prompt_token_ids[i, self.num_prompt_tokens[i]:] = self.vocab_size
695	        return prompt_token_ids_cpu_tensor.to(device=self.device,
696	                                              non_blocking=True)
697	
698	    def make_lora_inputs(
699	        self, num_scheduled_tokens: np.ndarray
700	    ) -> tuple[tuple[int, ...], tuple[int, ...], set[LoRARequest]]:
701	        """
702	        Given the num_scheduled_tokens for each request in the batch, return
703	        datastructures used to activate the current LoRAs.
704	        Returns:
705	            1. prompt_lora_mapping: A tuple of size self.num_reqs where,
706	               prompt_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for the ith prompt.
707	            2. token_lora_mapping: A tuple of size np.sum(num_scheduled_tokens)
708	               where, token_lora_mapping[i] is the LoRA id to use for ith token.
709	            3. lora_requests: Set of relevant LoRA requests.
710	        """
711	
712	        req_lora_mapping = self.request_lora_mapping[:self.num_reqs]
713	        prompt_lora_mapping = tuple(req_lora_mapping)
714	        token_lora_mapping = tuple(
715	            req_lora_mapping.repeat(num_scheduled_tokens))
716	        active_lora_requests: set[LoRARequest] = set(
717	            self.lora_id_to_lora_request.values())
718	
719	        return prompt_lora_mapping, token_lora_mapping, active_lora_requests
720	
721	    @property
722	    def num_reqs(self) -> int:
723	        return len(self.req_id_to_index)
724	
725	    @property
726	    def all_greedy(self) -> bool:
727	        return len(self.random_reqs) == 0
728	
729	    @property
730	    def all_random(self) -> bool:
731	        return len(self.greedy_reqs) == 0
732	
733	    @property
734	    def no_top_p(self) -> bool:
735	        return len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0
736	
737	    @property
738	    def no_top_k(self) -> bool:
739	        return len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0
740	
741	    @property
742	    def no_penalties(self) -> bool:
743	        return (len(self.presence_penalties_reqs) == 0
744	                and len(self.frequency_penalties_reqs) == 0
745	                and len(self.repetition_penalties_reqs) == 0)
746	
747	    @property
748	    def max_num_logprobs(self) -> Optional[int]:
749	        return max(self.num_logprobs.values()) if self.num_logprobs else None

1	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
2	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
3	
4	from typing import Optional
5	
6	import torch
7	import torch.nn as nn
8	
9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

23	class RejectionSampler(nn.Module):
24	    """
25	    The implementation strictly follows the algorithm described in
26	        https://arxiv.org/abs/2211.17192.
27	    However, we want to clarify the terminology used in the implementation:
28	    accepted tokens: tokens that are accepted based on the relationship
29	            between the "raw" draft and target probabilities.
30	    recovered tokens: tokens that are sampled based on the adjusted probability
31	        distribution, which is derived from both the draft and target
32	        probabilities.
33	    bonus tokens:
34	        If all proposed tokens are accepted, the bonus token is added to the
35	        end of the sequence. The bonus token is only sampled from the target
36	        probabilities. We pass in the bonus tokens instead of sampling them
37	        in the rejection sampler to allow for more flexibility in the
38	        sampling process. For example, we can use top_p, top_k sampling for
39	        bonus tokens, while spec decode does not support these sampling
40	        strategies.
41	    output tokens:
42	        Tokens are finally generated with the rejection sampler.
43	        output tokens = accepted tokens + recovered tokens + bonus tokens
44	    """
45	
46	    def forward(
47	        self,
48	        metadata: SpecDecodeMetadata,
49	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
50	        draft_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
51	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
52	        target_logits: torch.Tensor,
53	        # [batch_size, 1]
54	        bonus_token_ids: torch.Tensor,
55	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
56	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
57	        '''
58	        Args:
59	            metadata:
60	                Metadata for spec decoding.
61	            draft_probs (Optional[torch.Tensor]):
62	                Probability distribution for the draft tokens. Shape is
63	                [num_tokens, vocab_size]. Can be None if probabilities are
64	                not provided, which is the case for ngram spec decode.
65	            target_logits (torch.Tensor):
66	                Target model's logits probability distribution.
67	                Shape is [num_tokens, vocab_size]. Here, probabilities from
68	                different requests are flattened into a single tensor because
69	                this is the shape of the output logits.
70	                NOTE: `target_logits` can be updated in place to save memory.
71	            bonus_token_ids_tensor (torch.Tensor):
72	                A tensor containing bonus tokens. Shape is [batch_size, 1].
73	                Bonus tokens are added to the end of the sequence if all
74	                proposed tokens are accepted. We generate the bonus tokens
75	                outside of the rejection sampler with the default sampling
76	                strategy. It allows for more flexibility in the sampling
77	                process such as top_p, top_k sampling.
78	            sampling_metadata (vllm.v1.sample.metadata.SamplingMetadata):
79	                Additional metadata needed for sampling, such as temperature,
80	                top-k/top-p parameters, or other relevant information.
81	        Returns:
82	            output_token_ids (torch.Tensor):
83	                A tensor containing the final output token IDs.
84	        '''
85	        assert metadata.max_spec_len <= MAX_SPEC_LEN
86	        # [num_tokens, vocab_size]
87	        # NOTE(woosuk): `target_logits` can be updated in place inside the
88	        # `compute_probs` function.
89	        target_probs = compute_probs(
90	            target_logits,
91	            metadata.cu_num_draft_tokens,
92	            sampling_metadata,
93	        )
94	
95	        output_token_ids = rejection_sample(
96	            metadata.draft_token_ids,
97	            metadata.num_draft_tokens,
98	            metadata.max_spec_len,
99	            metadata.cu_num_draft_tokens,
100	            draft_probs,

444	def _greedy_sample(
445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
454	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
455	    Returns:
456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
460	    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
461	    sample_idx = 0
462	    results: SampleResultType = []
463	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
464	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
465	            results.append(([], []))
466	            continue
467	
468	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
469	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
470	        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
471	            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
472	        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
473	        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
474	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
475	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
476	    return results

479	def _random_sample(
480	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
481	    random_samples: torch.Tensor,
482	) -> SampleResultType:
483	    """Run random sampling on a given samples.
484	
485	    Args:
486	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
487	        random_samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The
488	            length of samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
489	            seq_group.do_sample is False.
490	    Returns:
491	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
492	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
493	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
494	    """
495	    # Find the maximum n value of the prompt phase requests.
496	    random_samples = random_samples.cpu()
497	    sample_idx = 0
498	    results: SampleResultType = []
499	    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
500	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
501	            results.append(([], []))
502	            continue
503	
504	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
505	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
506	        is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
507	        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
508	        if is_prompt:
509	            # Prompt phase.
510	            parent_ids = [0] * sampling_params.n
511	            next_token_ids = random_samples[
512	                sample_idx, :sampling_params.n].tolist()
513	        else:
514	            # Generation phase.
515	            parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
516	            next_token_ids = random_samples[sample_idx:sample_idx +
517	                                            num_parent_seqs, 0].tolist()
518	        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
519	        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
520	    return results

526	# probs will be modified in place, but this is fine, as we pass
527	# in a copy already.
528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None

528	def _multinomial(
529	    probs: torch.Tensor,
530	    num_samples: int,
531	    seq_groups: Optional[list[SequenceGroupToSample]] = None,
532	) -> torch.Tensor:
533	    if num_samples > 1:
534	        probs = probs.repeat_interleave(num_samples, dim=0)
535	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
536	    if seq_groups is None:
537	        q.exponential_()
538	    else:
539	        sample_idx = 0
540	        for seq_group in seq_groups:
541	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
542	            stride = len(seq_ids) * num_samples
543	            assert seq_group.generator is not None
544	            q[sample_idx:sample_idx +
545	              stride].exponential_(generator=seq_group.generator)
546	            sample_idx += stride
547	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=1).view(-1, num_samples)

558	        probs,

560	        top_ps,
561	    )
562	    return batch_next_token_ids.view(-1, num_samples)

13	def get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
14	    tokens: torch.Tensor,
15	    vocab_size: int,
16	    num_seqs: int,
17	) -> tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
18	    # Compute the bin counts for the tokens.
19	    # vocab_size + 1 for padding.
20	    bin_counts = torch.zeros((num_seqs, vocab_size + 1),
21	                             dtype=torch.long,
22	                             device=tokens.device)
23	    bin_counts.scatter_add_(1, tokens, torch.ones_like(tokens))
24	    bin_counts = bin_counts[:, :vocab_size]
25	    mask = bin_counts > 0
26	
27	    return bin_counts, mask

30	def apply_penalties(logits: torch.Tensor, prompt_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
31	                    output_tokens_tensor: torch.Tensor,
32	                    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor,
33	                    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor,
34	                    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
35	    """
36	    Applies penalties in place to the logits tensor
37	    logits : The input logits tensor of shape [num_seqs, vocab_size]
38	    prompt_tokens_tensor: A tensor containing the prompt tokens. The prompts 
39	        are padded to the maximum prompt length within the batch using 
40	        `vocab_size` as the padding value. The value `vocab_size` is used 
41	        for padding because it does not correspond to any valid token ID 
42	        in the vocabulary.
43	    output_tokens_tensor: The output tokens tensor.
44	    presence_penalties: The presence penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
45	    frequency_penalties: The frequency penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
46	    repetition_penalties: The repetition penalties of shape (num_seqs, )
47	    """
48	    num_seqs, vocab_size = logits.shape
49	    _, prompt_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(prompt_tokens_tensor,
50	                                                   vocab_size, num_seqs)
51	    output_bin_counts, output_mask = get_token_bin_counts_and_mask(
52	        output_tokens_tensor, vocab_size, num_seqs)
53	
54	    # Apply repetition penalties as a custom op

96	        return out.view(*x.shape[:-1], weight.shape[0])

105	        return torch.ops._C.weight_packed_linear(x, weight, bias, True)

113	    elif current_platform.is_cpu():
114	        return cpu_unquantized_gemm

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive report. Let me write it:

## Comprehensive Report: Sampling Parameters Threading in vLLM

Based on my thorough exploration of the vLLM codebase, here's how sampling parameters (particularly `top_k` and `top_p`) are threaded from the `SamplingParams` class to actual sampler execution:

---

### 1. **SamplingParams Class Definition**
**File:** `/vllm/sampling_params.py` (lines 118-245)

The `SamplingParams` class holds:
- **Line 212:** `top_p: float = 1.0` - cumulative probability threshold
- **Line 213:** `top_k: int = 0` - number of top tokens to consider

Key validation occurs in `_verify_args()` (lines 417-428):
- `top_p` must be in `(0, 1]` (line 418)
- `top_k` must be `>= 0` or `-1` to disable (lines 420-422)

When temperature is near zero (greedy sampling), both are reset to disable filtering (lines 379-381).

---

### 2. **V0 Architecture (Original vLLM)**

**Core Data Structure:** `SamplingMetadata` 
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 97-196)

**Intermediate Structure:** `SequenceGroupToSample`
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 20-62)

Contains per-sequence-group `SamplingParams`, which are later unpacked.

**Tensor Packing:** `SamplingTensors` 
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` (lines 372-597)

This is the critical intermediate step where parameters are extracted from `SamplingParams` and packed into batched tensors:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor      # <-- Packed from sampling_params.top_p
    top_ks: torch.Tensor      # <-- Packed from sampling_params.top_k
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
```

**Extraction Logic** (lines 387-495):
- Lines 415-416: Extract `top_p` and `top_k` from each `seq_group.sampling_params`
- Lines 419-420: Clamp `top_k` to vocab size
- Lines 443-460: Replicate values per sample index
- Returns both tensors and flags indicating whether filtering is needed (lines 426-430)

---

### 3. **V1 Architecture (New vLLM)**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

The V1 architecture is fundamentally different - it maintains batched tensors throughout the execution lifecycle rather than packing them per-step.

**Input Batch Storage** (lines 133-151):
```python
self.top_p = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
self.top_p_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), dtype=torch.float32, 
                                     device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
self.top_p_cpu = self.top_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
self.top_p_reqs: set[str] = set()  # Track which requests use top_p

self.top_k = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), dtype=torch.int32, device=device)
self.top_k_cpu_tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs,), dtype=torch.int32,
                                     device="cpu", pin_memory=pin_memory)
self.top_k_cpu = self.top_k_cpu_tensor.numpy()
self.top_k_reqs: set[str] = set()  # Track which requests use top_k
```

**Parameter Extraction** (lines 300-343):

When a request is added via `add_request()`:
```python
# Line 312: Extract top_p from SamplingParams
self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
if sampling_params.top_p < 1:
    self.top_p_reqs.add(req_id)

# Lines 315-320: Extract and clamp top_k
top_k = sampling_params.top_k
if 0 < top_k < self.vocab_size:
    self.top_k_reqs.add(req_id)
else:
    top_k = self.vocab_size
self.top_k_cpu[req_index] = top_k
```

**Metadata Creation** (lines 600-659):

Function `_make_sampling_metadata()` creates the sampling metadata for the current batch step:

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:  # V1 version
    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool
    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]      # <-- Only present if needed
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]      # <-- Only present if needed
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]
    # ... other fields
```

Key optimization (lines 642-659):
```python
return SamplingMetadata(
    temperature=temperature,
    all_greedy=self.all_greedy,
    all_random=self.all_random,
    top_p=None if self.no_top_p else self.top_p[:num_reqs],  # Slice to active requests
    top_k=None if self.no_top_k else self.top_k[:num_reqs],  # Slice to active requests
    generators=self.generators,
    max_num_logprobs=self.max_num_logprobs,
    # ...
)
```

Where:
- Line 734-735: `no_top_p` property returns `len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0`
- Line 738-739: `no_top_k` property returns `len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0`

This means if no requests need top_p/top_k filtering, `None` is passed to avoid computation.

---

### 4. **Sampler Execution**

**V0 Sampler:**
**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

The sampler receives `SamplingTensors` and uses them directly with broadcasting.

**V1 Sampler:**
**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` (lines 120-126)

```python
# Apply top_k and/or top_p.
random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
    logits,
    sampling_metadata.generators,
    sampling_metadata.top_k,    # <-- Passed directly as tensors
    sampling_metadata.top_p,    # <-- Passed directly as tensors
)
```

**Top-K/Top-P Application:**
**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

Three implementations:

1. **PyTorch Native** (lines 69-83): `apply_top_k_top_p()`
   - Lines 188-208: Sorts logits, applies masks, re-scatters
   - Handles both top-k and top-p with sophisticated masking

2. **CUDA with FlashInfer** (lines 85-107): Uses `flashinfer.sampling`
   - More optimized but requires FlashInfer library
   - Falls back to native on generator conflicts

3. **TPU Optimized** (lines 109-165): `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()`
   - Avoids slow `scatter` operations on TPU
   - Uses cutoff-based approach (lines 143-163)

**Key Function - apply_top_k_top_p():**
```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],     # Shape: (batch_size,)
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],     # Shape: (batch_size,)
) -> torch.Tensor:
```

Lines 180-208: Algorithm:
1. Sort logits in ascending order
2. Apply top-k mask (keep top k values only)
3. Apply top-p mask (keep until cumulative probability >= 1-p)
4. At least one token is always kept (line 203)
5. Re-scatter logits back to original order

---

### 5. **TPU Variant**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` (lines 22-125)

**TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:**
```python
@dataclass
class TPUSupportedSamplingMetadata:
    temperature: torch.Tensor = None
    min_p: torch.Tensor = None
    top_k: torch.Tensor = None      # <-- Same extraction
    top_p: torch.Tensor = None      # <-- Same extraction
```

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` (lines 50-76)

Similar flow but with TPU-specific implementations:
```python
random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
    logits,
    sampling_metadata.generators,
    sampling_metadata.top_k,
    sampling_metadata.top_p,
)
```

---

### 6. **Data Flow Summary**

```
SamplingParams (per-request)
    ↓ (added to batch)
InputBatch.top_k_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_k
InputBatch.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p
    ↓ (each step)
_make_sampling_metadata() slices active requests
    ↓
SamplingMetadata.top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]  # (num_active_reqs,)
SamplingMetadata.top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]  # (num_active_reqs,)
    ↓
Sampler.sample()
    ↓
TopKTopPSampler.forward_native/forward_cuda/forward_tpu()
    ↓
apply_top_k_top_p() / apply_top_k_top_p_tpu() / flashinfer.sampling
```

---

### 7. **Test Files**

**File:** `/tests/v1/sample/test_topk_topp_sampler.py`
- Tests `apply_top_k_top_p()` equivalence (lines 32-54)
- Tests FlashInfer implementation (lines 57-117)
- Compares with reference implementation

**File:** `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampling_params_e2e.py`
- End-to-end sampling parameter validation

**File:** `/tests/v1/sample/test_sampler.py`
- Full sampler integration tests

---

### 8. **Key Differences: V0 vs V1**

| Aspect | V0 | V1 |
|--------|----|----|
| Storage | Packed per-step in `SamplingTensors` | Persistent `InputBatch` buffers |
| Slicing | Done per-step in `from_sampling_metadata()` | Done once per request in `add_request()` |
| None Handling | Separate `do_top_p_top_k` flags | `Optional[torch.Tensor]` with `no_top_p`/`no_top_k` properties |
| Device | CPU tensors with async H2D transfer | CPU + GPU tensors with non-blocking transfer |
| Optimization | Returns boolean flags | Sets None to skip computation entirely |

---

### 9. **Critical Files Summary**

| File Path | Lines | Purpose |
|-----------|-------|---------|
| `/vllm/sampling_params.py` | 212-213, 417-428 | SamplingParams definition & validation |
| `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 20-62, 372-597 | V0 SequenceGroupToSample & SamplingTensors |
| `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py` | 133-151, 312-320, 600-659 | V1 InputBatch storage & metadata creation |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py` | 13-43 | V1 SamplingMetadata definition |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 120-126 | V1 Sampler calls TopKTopPSampler |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 69-296 | Core top-k/top-p implementations |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/sampler.py` | 50-76 | TPU-specific sampler |
| `/vllm/v1/sample/tpu/metadata.py` | 22-125 | TPU-specific metadata |

This architecture enables efficient batched sampling while maintaining per-request customization through tensor indexing and optional field handling.

- Line 738-739: `no_top_k` property returns `len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0`

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,

    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],     # Shape: (batch_size,)

```

Lines 180-208: Algorithm:
1. Sort logits in ascending order
2. Apply top-k mask (keep top k values only)
3. Apply top-p mask (keep until cumulative probability >= 1-p)

## Summary of vLLM Sampling and Filtering Logic

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase to understand how top_p and top_k filtering are applied to logits and probabilities. Here are my findings:

### 1. Main Sampler Files

**Primary sampler implementations:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Main V0 sampler (legacy)

- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - V1 top-k/top-p implementation

**Supporting metadata and parameter files:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/vllm/sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams class definition
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - SamplingMetadata and SamplingTensors definitions
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` - apply_penalties function

**CUDA kernels:**
- `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775397088141-icp01u/csrc/sampler.cu` - Repetition penalty kernel

---

### 2. How Top-K Filtering Is Implemented

**V0 Implementation** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, lines 398-424):
The `_apply_top_k_top_p()` function:
```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Key mechanics:**
- Sorts logits in ascending order (line 403)
- Calculates the position of the k-th largest logit (line 406)
- Creates a boolean mask for tokens below the k-th largest value (line 409)
- Sets those tokens to -inf in the sorted logits (line 410)

**V1 Implementation** (`vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 211-233):
The `apply_top_k_only()` function provides an optimized version without full sorting:
```python
def apply_top_k_only(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
    max_top_k = k.max()
    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### 3. How Top-P (Nucleus Sampling) Filtering Is Implemented

**V0 Implementation** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, lines 412-418):
```python
    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
```

**Key mechanics:**
- Converts sorted logits to probabilities via softmax (line 413)
- Computes cumulative sum of probabilities (line 414)
- Masks tokens where cumulative probability is less than (1 - p), keeping only the top-p mass (line 415)
- Ensures at least one token remains (line 417)
- Sets masked tokens to -inf (line 418)

**V1 Implementation** (`vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 197-204):
```python
    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p.
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        # at least one
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
```

---

### 4. Order of Operations for Sampling Filters

**V0 Sampler** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, forward method, lines 269-305):

1. **Line 269**: Apply min_tokens penalty
2. **Lines 272-277**: Apply presence and frequency penalties via `apply_penalties()`
3. **Lines 281-282**: Apply temperature scaling (divide logits by temperature)
4. **Lines 284-286**: Apply top-k and top-p filtering (if enabled and no FlashInfer)
5. **Lines 288-289**: Apply min_p filtering (if enabled)
6. **Lines 293-295**: Compute probabilities and log-probabilities via softmax
7. **Lines 298-305**: Sample tokens via `_sample()` function

**V1 Sampler** (`vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`, forward method, lines 25-77):

1. **Line 43**: Apply allowed token ids masking
2. **Line 45**: Apply bad words exclusion
3. **Lines 47-49**: Apply logits processors (non-argmax invariant)
4. **Line 52**: Apply penalties (min_tokens, frequency, etc.)
5. **Line 54**: Call `sample()` method, which applies:
   - Greedy sampling (argmax) if temperature < eps
   - OR temperature scaling + argmax-invariant logits processors + top-k/top-p + random sampling

### 5. GPU Kernel Calls for Sampling

**Repetition Penalties Kernel** (`csrc/sampler.cu`, lines 14-45):
- CUDA kernel `apply_repetition_penalties_kernel` called via `apply_repetition_penalties()`
- Applies in-place logit modifications for tokens in prompt/output history
- Uses grid-stride loops with tiling for efficiency

**FlashInfer Integration**:
- V0 uses optional FlashInfer via `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling` (lines 26-33 of sampler.py)
- V1 uses FlashInfer in `forward_cuda()` method of `TopKTopPSampler` (lines 85-107 of topk_topp_sampler.py)
- FlashInfer functions called:
  - `flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs()` (line 284)
  - `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs()` (line 289)
  - `flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits()` (line 293)

---

### 6. Intermediate Data Structures Carrying Sampling Parameters

**SamplingParams** (`vllm/sampling_params.py`, lines 118-200):
- Defines sampling parameters: `top_p`, `top_k`, `min_p`, `temperature`, penalties
- Passed by user/API to control sampling behavior

**SamplingTensors** (`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, lines 373-597):
- Created from `SamplingParams` via `from_sampling_metadata()` classmethod
- Contains GPU tensors with per-token sampling parameters:
  - `top_ps`: tensor of top_p values for each token
  - `top_ks`: tensor of top_k values for each token
  - `temperatures`: tensor of temperature values
  - `min_ps`: tensor of min_p values
  - `presence_penalties`, `frequency_penalties`, `repetition_penalties`
  - `prompt_tokens`, `output_tokens`: for penalty application
- Uses pinned memory for fast GPU transfer (lines 545-596)

**SequenceGroupToSample** (`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, lines 19-62):
- Groups sequences with same sampling parameters
- Contains `sampling_params` reference and indices for sampling

**SamplingMetadata** (`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, lines 97-196):
- Batches multiple sequence groups
- Contains `seq_groups` list with sampling parameters
- Stores selected token indices and categorized sample indices by SamplingType

---

### 7. Sample Result Processing

**_sample_with_torch()** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, lines 614-735):
- Categorizes sequence groups by `SamplingType` (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
- For GREEDY: uses `torch.argmax()` on logprobs
- For RANDOM: uses `_multinomial()` function which:
  - Generates exponential random variables (via `q.exponential_()`)
  - Applies Gumbel-max trick: `argmax(log(probs) + log(q))`
  - Avoids CPU-GPU sync unlike `torch.multinomial()`

**Result collection** (lines 716-735):
- Returns either Pythonized results (immediate) or deferred args for multi-step execution
- Includes sampled token ids tensor for multi-step decoding

- Groups sequences with same sampling parameters
- Contains `sampling_params` reference and indices for sampling

**SamplingMetadata** (`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`, lines 97-196):
- Batches multiple sequence groups
- Contains `seq_groups` list with sampling parameters
- Stores selected token indices and categorized sample indices by SamplingType

---

### 7. Sample Result Processing

**_sample_with_torch()** (`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`, lines 614-735):
- Categorizes sequence groups by `SamplingType` (GREEDY, RANDOM, RANDOM_SEED)
- For GREEDY: uses `torch.argmax()` on logprobs
- For RANDOM: uses `_multinomial()` function which:
  - Generates exponential random variables (via `q.exponential_()`)
  - Applies Gumbel-max trick: `argmax(log(probs) + log(q))`
  - Avoids CPU-GPU sync unlike `torch.multinomial()`

**Result collection** (lines 716-735):
- Returns either Pythonized results (immediate) or deferred args for multi-step execution
- Includes sampled token ids tensor for multi-step decoding

---

### Key Code Locations Summary

| Component | File Path | Lines |
|-----------|-----------|-------|
| Top-k/top-p filtering (V0) | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 398-424 |
| Top-k/top-p filtering (V1) | `vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` | 168-233 |
| Min_p filtering | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 427-441 |
| Penalty application | `vllm/model_executor/layers/utils.py` | 30-63 |
| Sampling tensor creation | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | 386-597 |
| V0 main forward | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 225-350 |
| V1 main forward | `vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` | 25-77 |
| Multinomial sampling | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | 528-547 |
| CUDA penalties | `csrc/sampler.cu` | 14-45 |

134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat

140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.

148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens

153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.

162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.

168	            output text. Defaults to False.

170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.

172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated

175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.

class SamplingParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Sampling parameters for text generation.

    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.

    Args:
        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
            tokens.
        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
            repeat tokens.
        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
            the model to repeat tokens.
        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
            the stop tokens are special tokens.
        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
            can complete the sequence.
        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
            output text. Defaults to False.
        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
            a first argument.
        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
            Defaults to None.
        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
            sampling implementations.
    """

    n: int = 1
    best_of: Optional[int] = None
    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None

ignore_eos: bool = False
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
    min_tokens: int = 0
    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
    detokenize: bool = True
    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE

    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
    # They are set in post_init.
    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)

    # Fields used to construct logits processors

    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None

    # Fields used for bad words
    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None

    @staticmethod
    def from_optional(
        n: Optional[int] = 1,
        best_of: Optional[int] = None,

        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,

        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,

        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,

        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,

        if logit_bias is not None:

400	            if self.best_of < self.n:

403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")

415	            raise ValueError(

417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0

427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "

442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")

451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)

455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")

# between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None

399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425

427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.

144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
